successCriteria.description: Create src/modules/ping/ping.model.ts exporting the PingResponse interface with a single `status: string` property — a stateless value object with no identity or lifecycle.
Create src/modules/ping/ping.service.interface.ts exporting the IPingService interface with a single method `getPing(): PingResponse`, importing PingResponse from ./ping.model.
Create src/modules/ping/ping.service.ts exporting the PingService class that implements IPingService, with getPing() returning the literal `{ status: 'ok' }`.
All three files follow the exact structural pattern of the existing uptime module: model → service interface → service, with identical import/export conventions and file layout.
The ping module is fully self-contained — zero imports from any other module in the project (no cross-module dependencies).

constraints: Must follow the exact structural pattern from src/modules/uptime/: uptime.model.ts → uptime.service.interface.ts → uptime.service.ts, with identical import/export conventions, file naming, and code layout.
PingResponse is a stateless value object — no identity, no lifecycle, no persistence, no database access, no repository layer.
The ping module must have zero dependencies on any other module — it is a self-contained leaf module.
Use ES module syntax (import/export), never require().
GP-001 (Repository pattern): N/A — no database access in this phase.
GP-002 (Audit records): N/A — no state-changing operations in this phase.
GP-003 (Input validation): N/A — no request body or query parameters in this phase.
GP-004 (No sensitive data in logs): Satisfied — the response contains only the literal `{"status": "ok"}`.
GP-005 (RBAC enforcement): N/A — public health-check endpoint; no RBAC needed.

GP-006 (Error handling): N/A in this phase — the service method is synchronous and infallible (returns a hardcoded literal). Error handling will be addressed in Phase 2 when the route handler is created.
outOfScope: Phase 2 — Ping route, barrel export, and app registration: Creating ping.routes.ts (Fastify route handler for GET /ping), index.ts (barrel export), and registering the route in src/app.ts.
Any persistence layer — no tables, no repositories, no database access for the ping module.
Any integration with other modules (employee, leave, balance, policy, notification, audit, uptime, status).
Test files — unit tests for the ping module are not part of this phase.
